metadata.title: 1 Room Kitchen (1RK)
metadata.description: Entry-level residential unit with a separate kitchen and a single multipurpose room. The bedroom and living functions share one room; the kitchen is physically separated.

body.purpose: Provide an affordable entry-level unit where the kitchen is separated from the living-sleeping room, giving the occupant a functional cooking space without the cost of a full 1BHK. Common in high-density urban rental markets.
body.planning_philosophy: The defining characteristic of a 1RK is the physical separation of the kitchen from the main room. This single boundary — a wall and a door — transforms the spatial experience from a studio into a dwelling with distinct functional zones. The plan must honour that boundary while keeping the unit compact and efficient.

occupancy.occupant_profile: single working professional or migrant worker
occupancy.lifestyle_notes: Occupant cooks regularly. The kitchen separation is a primary quality-of-life requirement — cooking odours and clutter must not enter the sleeping area.

required_spaces.name: living_sleeping_room
required_spaces.notes: Single multipurpose room accommodating bed, seating, and storage. Must have a door that closes it off from the kitchen and entry.

required_spaces.name: kitchen
required_spaces.notes: Fully enclosed kitchen with hob, sink, and storage. Must have a door separating it from the main room. This separation is the defining feature of a 1RK.

required_spaces.name: bathroom
required_spaces.notes: Combined WC, wash basin, and shower. Must be fully enclosed and independently ventilated.

required_spaces.name: entry_passage
required_spaces.notes: Entry zone that distributes to the kitchen, bathroom, and main room without passing through any of them.

optional_spaces.name: dry_balcony
optional_spaces.notes: Laundry and drying. Accessed from kitchen or passage, not from the main room.

optional_spaces.name: utility_alcove
optional_spaces.notes: Washing machine point and mop sink. Can be integrated into the kitchen or passage if space is constrained.

space_relationships.notes: Kitchen is accessed from the entry passage, not through the main room.
Main room is accessed from the entry passage with a closable door.
The wall and door between kitchen and main room is the defining spatial boundary of a 1RK. Must not be open-plan.
Bathroom accessed from passage, not from the main room or kitchen.
Wet room and food preparation must not share a plumbing wall on the same face.

circulation.rule: The entry passage must distribute to all three spaces — kitchen, bathroom, and main room — without requiring passage through any one of them to reach another.
circulation.rationale: Prevents the kitchen from becoming a circulation corridor, which would defeat the purpose of the kitchen separation.

circulation.rule: The kitchen door must swing into the kitchen, not into the passage.
circulation.rationale: Passage widths in compact units are typically 3–4 ft. An outward-swinging door creates a collision hazard.

circulation.rule: The main room must have a minimum 3 ft clear path from door to window.
circulation.rationale: Maintains the sense of depth and prevents the room from feeling like a storage space.

ventilation.notes: Minimum one operable window. Cross-ventilation strongly preferred.
Ducted exhaust fan mandatory. If natural ventilation is available, it supplements but does not replace mechanical extraction.
Exhaust fan mandatory. Minimum 6 air changes per hour. Duct to external face.
Borrowed light and ventilation from adjacent spaces is acceptable if passage is short.

lighting.notes: Window must face an open aspect. The main room carries the entire natural light burden of the unit.
Under-cabinet task lighting mandatory. Natural light preferred but not required given the enclosed nature of the kitchen.

engineering.constraint: Minimum floor-to-floor height 10 ft to allow 9 ft clear ceiling after slab and finish.
Kitchen and bathroom must share a common wet wall or be on adjacent walls of the same wet core to minimise pipe runs.
Dedicated 20A circuit for kitchen appliances. Minimum 3 double outlets in main room.
Smoke detector in main room. Heat detector in kitchen. Both interconnected with building fire alarm.
Kitchen must meet minimum habitable room standards if it exceeds 70 sqft — adequate ventilation and natural light required.

construction.guideline: Kitchen wall separating it from the main room should achieve minimum STC 45 to contain cooking noise and odour.
Kitchen walls to be tiled to full height behind hob and sink. Washable paint on remaining kitchen walls.
Full-height overhead cabinets in kitchen. Built-in wardrobe in main room minimum 4 ft wide.
Solid-core door between kitchen and passage to contain cooking odours. Hollow-core acceptable for bathroom and main room.

constraints: Kitchen must be physically separated from the main room by a wall and door

validation_rules.condition: kitchen is present and separated from living_sleeping_room by a wall and door
entry_passage distributes to kitchen, bathroom, and main room independently
bathroom is present and fully enclosed
living_sleeping_room has at least one natural light source
